text: Jarvis, what are the calendar items for Thursday?
title: Morning Greeting

text: Morning Mike! Today is Thursday, August 15. You have an architecture review at 11 AM, and pediatrician checkup for Lily.
title: Jarvis Morning Briefing

text: Quick log: Sarah and I are discussing screen-time rules for the kids. Ethan wants to play Roblox instead of doing his reading. They keep us on our toes.
title: Parenting Update

text: Understood. Good planning. A 'homework first, then Roblox' rule helps balance screen time. Parenting is definitely a journey.
title: Jarvis Parenting Response

text: Quick log: Soldering custom sensors in the garage. Curiosity and Perseverance RTG decay rates differ. Really satisfying to build this myself.
title: Neurodivergent Update

text: Understood. I've indexed the research notes. MMRTG heat management is complex. I've archived your propulsion notes. I've saved your custom configurations.
title: Jarvis Neurodivergent Response

text: Ethan is working on a school assignment at the table. He wants to build a clay model of Saturn for his science project. He is growing up so fast.
title: Kids School Update

text: Got it, Mike. That's wonderful. We have clay and foam rings in the garage storage shelves that will be perfect. I've logged this academic update.
title: Jarvis Kids School Response

text: Just wanted to mention that working on the roadmap slides for Q4. Anika Patel did a deep competitor analysis showing MyChart has patient onboarding. Greg is not going to like this, but we have to push back.
title: Work Update

text: Got it, Mike. Here is what I recommend. This competitor data is a powerful lever. You can use it to justify keeping patient onboarding in the MVP. Let's monitor the error rates over the next sprint.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Quick log: Tracking my weekly fitness metrics. I ran 5 miles but my knee felt tight. Hope the allergies calm down.
title: Health Update

text: Logged that. Sleep quality is so important. Great run! Remember to stretch your hamstrings to help the knee. Keep stretching that knee!
title: Jarvis Health Response

text: Hey Jarvis, Analyzing the system logs from yesterday's release. We got beta feedback from clinics suggesting they need patient onboarding immediately. We need to get this sorted before the Baylor pilot starts.
title: Work Update

text: Logged that. I can help with that. We should prioritize these clinic requests. Let's create a dedicated sub-task in the CareConnect backlog. I can add this optimization to the backlog.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Quick update: Reviewing our household budget today. They requested our W-2 and bank statements. Will talk this over with Sarah.
title: Finance Update

text: Interesting. I can run some projections for you. I have prepared a checklist of documents in your shared folder. I'll update the budget sheet.
title: Jarvis Finance Response

text: Quick update: Lily had a tough time at bedtime tonight. Ethan and Marcus Nguyen are playing in our backyard. They keep us on our toes.
title: Parenting Update

text: Good planning. Playdates are great for Ethan. I can add snack shopping to the Target list. Parenting is definitely a journey.
title: Jarvis Parenting Response

text: Quick update: Spent hours calibrating the telescope mount. I quantized a local LLM to run in memory. My ADHD hyperfocus really kicked in.
title: Neurodivergent Update

text: Your hyperfocus projects are impressive. Quantizing 70B models is heavy coding. I've saved settings in your scratchpad. Let me know if you want to run testing scripts.
title: Jarvis Neurodivergent Response

text: Sarah attended the parent-teacher conference today. He wants to build a clay model of Saturn for his science project. He is growing up so fast.
title: Kids School Update

text: Got it, Mike. That's wonderful. We have clay and foam rings in the garage storage shelves that will be perfect. I'll update the calendar reminder.
title: Jarvis Kids School Response

text: Hey Jarvis, I'm reviewing the secure messaging specs for Vertex Health. Greg Holloway wants to drop secure messaging to save time, but Anika thinks it is essential. Hopefully we can align on this during the stakeholder review.
title: Work Update

text: Understood. I've noted that. I suggest a phased rollout. Shipping a basic version of secure messaging now and iterating later is a good compromise. I can compile the competitor metrics if needed.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Quick log: Went for a run around the neighborhood. Ethan's grass allergies are acting up again. Will keep monitoring the metrics.
title: Health Update

text: Sure thing! I've logged your vitals. I've set a calendar slot for the clinic and added meds refills. I'll monitor the sleep metrics tonight.
title: Jarvis Health Response

text: Quick log: I'm reviewing the scheduling portal specs for Vertex Health. VP Greg Holloway is complaining about the infrastructure costs for CareConnect. Let's see if we can optimize the code first.
title: Work Update

text: Logged that. I've noted that. We can present a cost-benefit analysis showing that dropping these optimizations will cost more in support tickets. I can add this optimization to the backlog.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Planning for future education costs. The refi quote saves us about $250 a month. Every bit of savings helps.
title: Finance Update

text: Smart financial planning. That's $3,000 annually. It could cover the Colorado trip or 529 plans. Ready to calculate more rates.
title: Jarvis Finance Response

text: Jarvis, logging today's journal. Lots of communication with Greg and Anika today. Logged my running mileage and stretched my knee. CareConnect prep is moving along. Ready for sleep.
title: Evening Journal Entry

text: Journal logged, Mike. Rest well and I'll see you tomorrow.
title: Jarvis Journal Summary
